# iproute_route (Resource)

Manages a routing table entry (ip route).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Basic route via gateway
resource "iproute_route" "subnet" {
  destination = "192.168.1.0/24"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.254"
  device      = "eth0"
}

# Default route
resource "iproute_route" "default" {
  destination = "default"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.1"
  device      = "eth0"
}

# Route with metric
resource "iproute_route" "with_metric" {
  destination = "10.1.0.0/16"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.1"
  device      = "eth0"
  metric      = 100
}

# Route in a custom table
resource "iproute_route" "custom_table" {
  destination = "172.16.0.0/12"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.1"
  device      = "eth0"
  table       = 100
}

# Blackhole route
resource "iproute_route" "blackhole" {
  destination = "10.99.0.0/16"
  type        = "blackhole"
}

# Unreachable route
resource "iproute_route" "unreachable" {
  destination = "10.98.0.0/16"
  type        = "unreachable"
}

# Prohibit route
resource "iproute_route" "prohibit" {
  destination = "10.97.0.0/16"
  type        = "prohibit"
}

# Route with MTU
resource "iproute_route" "with_mtu" {
  destination = "10.2.0.0/24"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.1"
  device      = "eth0"
  mtu         = 1400
}

# Route with source address
resource "iproute_route" "with_source" {
  destination = "10.3.0.0/24"
  gateway     = "10.0.0.1"
  device      = "eth0"
  source      = "10.0.0.5"
}

# IPv6 route
resource "iproute_route" "ipv6" {
  destination = "fd01::/64"
  gateway     = "fd00::1"
  device      = "eth0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destination` (String) Destination in CIDR notation or 'default'.

### Optional

- `advmss` (Number) Advertised MSS.
- `device` (String) Output device name.
- `encap` (Block, Optional) Route encapsulation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--encap))
- `gateway` (String) Gateway IP address.
- `metric` (Number) Route metric/priority.
- `mtu` (Number) Route MTU.
- `multipath` (Attributes List) ECMP multipath routes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--multipath))
- `nexthop_id` (Number) Nexthop ID.
- `onlink` (Boolean) Gateway is on-link even if not in any connected subnet.
- `protocol` (String) Route protocol.
- `scope` (String) Route scope.
- `source` (String) Source address for route.
- `table` (Number) Routing table ID.
- `type` (String) Route type (unicast, blackhole, unreachable, prohibit).

### Read-Only

- `family` (String) Address family.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--encap"></a>
### Nested Schema for `encap`

Optional:

- `labels` (List of String) Labels.
- `type` (String) Encap type (mpls, seg6, etc).


<a id="nestedatt--multipath"></a>
### Nested Schema for `multipath`

Optional:

- `device` (String)
- `gateway` (String)
- `weight` (Number)
